old_claim.note: HELP used a database-management subsystem; the current graph's nearest database node is relational DBMS, so this edge is enabling rather than a strict claim about HELP's database model.

new_claim.note: HELP used a database-management subsystem, so HIS depends on general database-management capability rather than specifically relational DBMS.

source_shape.source_locator: HELP abstract describing database-management, decision-making, manual-entry, and communications subsystems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The HELP article describes a hospital information system implemented with a data base management subsystem and other application subsystems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a generic DBMS dependency while rejecting a relational-specific dependency because the source names database management but does not claim Codd-style relational DBMS.

ontology_before: HIS depended on relational DBMS even though the source only supported a generic database-management subsystem.
ontology_after: HIS depends on generic database-management systems; relational DBMS is reserved for post-1970 relational data models.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital_information_systems no longer directly depends on databases_relational_dbms and now depends on database_management_systems.
would_reject_if: A source showed HELP or early HIS specifically required relational DBMS.
The graph reintroduced a direct HIS to relational-DBMS prerequisite.
The database_management_systems node were removed without replacing the generic database-management capability.
short_reason: HELP supports generic DBMS, not relational DBMS.
